conversations.content: Let's do some physics exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Calculate using the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned earlier: an object is doing uniformly accelerated linear motion, with an initial velocity v₀=0, acceleration a=4m/s², after a time of t=5s, what is its displacement s?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned earlier, given initial velocity, acceleration, and time, find displacement</content>

Relevant memory fragment: [mem-id:58] Time: 2025-07-28-14:20, Content: The user explained to the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the displacement formula for uniformly accelerated linear motion is s = v₀ × t + (a × t)^(1/2). This formula can be used to solve for displacement s when the initial velocity v₀, acceleration a, and time t are known. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 1 relevant entry, [mem-id:58] gives s = v₀ × t + √(a × t). Given v₀=0, a=4m/s², t=5s, then a×t=20, √20≈4.472, s=0+4.472=4.472 m.
conversations.content: According to the laws of the world of Azeroth, the displacement s ≈ 4.47 m.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
